title: cyclamen coum subsp. coum
intro: Cyclamen coum subsp. coum, also known as the early-purple cyclamen, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by heart-shaped leaves and delicate, nodding flowers in shades of purple and white. The roots and leaves of Cyclamen coum subsp. coum contain glycosides, including cyclamin, which has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties to treat various health issues. The plant has been used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory problems, such as coughs and bronchitis, and its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties have been employed to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any plant-based remedies.
